years.imagePrompt: A newborn baby, just born, peaceful and innocent

questionVariants.question: <Name> won't stop crying. What do you do?
samples: I check every possible need and hold them until they settle
I pace the room, hum softly, and stay patient
I swaddle them, turn on white noise, and go into survival mode

questionVariants.question: <Name> is sick for the first time. What do you do?
samples: Call the doctor, monitor everything, and stay close
Keep them comfortable, hydrated, and watched all night
Panic internally, act calm externally, and follow the pediatrician

questionVariants.question: You're exhausted. <Name> needs you. What do you do?
samples: Pick them up anyway and do what needs doing
Cry a little, make coffee, and keep going
Become weirdly efficient and power through the fog

questionVariants.question: <Name> comes home and asks where babies come from. What do you tell them?
samples: The simple honest version they can handle
Love first, biology second, and no weird panic
A careful answer that is truthful but age-appropriate

questionVariants.question: <Name> tells you they hate you for the first time. What do you do?
samples: Stay calm and tell them I love them anyway
Let the storm pass, then talk once we're both steady
Refuse to escalate and wait for the real feeling underneath

questionVariants.question: <Name> breaks something important to you. What do you do?
samples: Take a breath and remind myself they're more important
Make them help clean it up and talk about honesty
Choose grace, but make sure they understand why it mattered

questionVariants.question: It's time to teach <Name> to ride a bike. How do you do it?
samples: Run beside them until they believe they can do it
Teach balance first, then let go before they notice
Give clear instructions, one push of confidence, and no speech

questionVariants.question: <Name> wants to quit school. What do you say?
samples: School is hard, but we're not quitting on hard things
Tell me what's really going on before we decide anything
No, but we are going to figure out why you feel this way

questionVariants.question: <Name> asks if you've ever done something you're ashamed of. What do you say?
samples: Yes, and I tell them what it taught me
Answer honestly without making it all about me
Tell the truth carefully so they learn something real

questionVariants.question: <Name> comes home with a black eye and won't say a word. What do you do?
samples: Sit beside them quietly until they're ready to talk
Check if they're safe first, then ask direct questions
Document everything and make it clear this won't be ignored

questionVariants.question: <Name> asks if they can stop doing the thing you've always made them do. What do you say?
samples: Ask why this matters so much to them
Tell them maybe, if they can argue their case honestly
Push back first, then actually listen

questionVariants.question: <Name>'s teacher says they've been acting out. What do you do?
samples: Ask for specifics before I judge my child
Talk to them privately and look for what's underneath it
Assume there's a reason and refuse to treat this as random

questionVariants.question: <Name> was caught shoplifting. What's the punishment?
samples: They return it, apologize, and earn back trust slowly
Grounded, job added, and every cent accounted for
A consequence harsh enough that they won't romanticize this

questionVariants.question: <Name> comes home drunk for the first time. What do you do?
samples: Make sure they're safe first and lecture second
Water, bucket, honesty, then consequences in the morning
Stay calm enough to scare them more than yelling would

questionVariants.question: You find something in <Name>'s room you weren't supposed to see. What do you do?
samples: Put it back and wait for them to bring it up
Confront them directly, but not in a rage
Take a beat so I don't turn one bad discovery into a worse conversation

questionVariants.question: <Name>'s first relationship just ended. They're devastated. What do you tell them?
samples: Heartbreak doesn't mean you were foolish to love
This pain passes, but what you learn from it stays
Grieve it fully and don't perform being fine for me

questionVariants.question: <Name> totals the car. Nobody's hurt. What do you do?
samples: Thank God they're alive, then deal with the consequences
Stay calm, call insurance, and make them own every part of it
No screaming at the scene, but this year is about accountability

questionVariants.question: <Name> asks you if you've ever had your heart broken. What do you say?
samples: Yes, and I tell them what it made me do wrong
Yes, and surviving it mattered more than winning it
Yes, and I admit I handled it badly at first

questionVariants.question: <Name> got into college but not the one you were hoping for. They're watching your face. What do you say?
samples: I'm proud of you before my face says anything stupid
The school matters less than what you do there
Take one breath, kill my ego, and choose their future over mine

questionVariants.question: <Name> wants to take a gap year to chase a high school love. What do you say?
samples: Show me a real plan, not just a feeling
Love is not a plan unless you can defend it like one
Maybe, if you can prove this is bigger than panic and fantasy

questionVariants.question: <Name> asks what you wish you'd done differently. What do you say?
samples: Taken more risks while I still had time
Been kinder sooner and less proud about being right
Learned earlier that regret compounds faster than money
